game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Boxville 2** Boxville 2 is a narrative-driven point-and-click puzzle adventure game where players control a red can character, navigating hand-drawn environments to solve a city-wide mystery. Players interact with the world by tapping on objects and characters to trigger animations, collect items into an inventory, or initiate one of dozens of logic puzzles and mini-games. Progression is linear and story-based, requiring players to solve sequential puzzles, such as re-arranging gears to activate a mechanism or decoding visual patterns, to unlock new areas and advance the plot of finding a missing friend and fixing misfired fireworks. The game features no spoken or written dialogue; instead, character intentions and puzzle clues are conveyed through animated thought bubbles and visual sketches, with difficulty scaling through increasingly complex multi-step puzzles and environmental challenges.
game_info.description: Guide a red can character through a hand-drawn city to solve a mystery. Explore interconnected scenes, collect items, and solve visual puzzles to progress. Use your inventory items on objects in the environment to unlock new areas. Complete all puzzles across multiple locations to reach the final scene and win the game. No text—everything is told through animated visuals and thought bubbles.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move character left/right through the scene
Space: Interact with highlighted objects or characters
Z: Open/close inventory to view collected items
Shift: Use selected inventory item on highlighted object
ESC: Pause/unpause game
R: Return to start screen
ENTER: Start game from title screen

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic movement and interaction mechanics. Validates that the player can move between positions, interact with objects, and trigger basic puzzle elements without errors.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move the character randomly across the scene, interact with various interactable objects, and attempt to open/close inventory. This tests the fundamental game controls and collision detection.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Character moves smoothly, interactions trigger properly, inventory opens/closes correctly, and no errors occur during basic gameplay. Test runs for a set duration without crashes.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests the complete game progression from start to finish. Implements an optimal strategy to solve all puzzles in sequence and reach the win condition.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Follow the correct sequence: interact with specific objects to collect items, use items in the right locations to solve puzzles, progress through all scenes in order, and complete the final puzzle to win.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Successfully completes all puzzles, collects all necessary items, progresses through all scenes, and reaches GAME_OVER_WIN state, demonstrating that the game is completable.
